description_long: This church features a symmetrical facade with two tall bell towers capped by domes and spires, flanking a central entrance. The exterior walls are finished in a light, stone-like material with subtle decorative elements, including arched windows and ornate trim. The main entrance is accentuated by a pair of green doors and two windows above, with a small circular window and a cross motif on the upper facade. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in reddish-brown tiles, extending the full length of the building. The side elevations display a series of evenly spaced arched windows and additional entryways, suggesting a spacious interior. The overall design is reminiscent of Baroque or Colonial church architecture, with a focus on verticality and decorative detail.
description: A grand, symmetrical church with twin domed bell towers, arched windows, and a steep red-tiled roof, finished in light stone with ornate details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, twin bell towers with domes, decorative facade, and central cross motif.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the building, series of arched windows, and one bell tower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, roofline, and both bell towers from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with arched windows and the second bell tower visible.
